blocks:
Sūrat al-Ḥashr (The Exile)

Medinan, and it is 24 verses

(١) سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. (1) Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth glorifies Allah, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٢) هُوَ الَّذِي أَخْرَجَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ لِأَوَّلِ الْحَشْرِ ** (2) It is He who expelled the faithless among the People of the Book from their homes at the first mustering. Meaning: at the first exile (جلاء) of theirs to Syria (الشّام). Their final mustering (حشر) to it will be during the tremor (الرّجفة), as the indication of this passed in Sūrat al-Dukhān. And al-Ḥashr (الحشر) is the expulsion and gathering from one place to another.

In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn 'Abbās: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Go out." They said: "To where?" He said: "To the land of the gathering (أرض المحشر)."

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Ḥasan al-Mujtabā عليه السلام in a tradition: [He mentioned] the King of Rome (ملك الرّوم), then Allah sends forth a fire from the East and a fire from the West, and two strong winds follow them, so the people are gathered (يُحْشَر) near the Rock of Jerusalem (صخرة بيت المقدّس). And al-Qummi said: The reason for this was that there were three clans (أبطن) of Jews in Medina: Banū al-Naḍīr (بنى النّضير), Qurayẓah (وقريظة), and Qaynuqā' (وقينقاع). There was a covenant and a term between them and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but they broke their covenant. The reason for Banū al-Naḍīr breaking their covenant was that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم came to them to borrow (يسلفهم) the blood money (دية) for two men whom one of his companions had killed by treachery (غيلة)—meaning, He was seeking a loan (يستقرض). He intended to visit Ka‘b ibn al-Ashraf (كعب بن الأشرف). When He entered upon Ka‘b, [Ka‘b] said: "Welcome, O Abū al-Qāsim, and greetings!" And he stood up as if he were preparing food for Him, but he spoke to himself, intending to kill the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and follow up with his companions. Then Gabriel descended and informed Him of this. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned to Medina and said to Muḥammad ibn Maslamah al-Anṣārī (محمّد بن مسيلمة الانصَارى): "Go to Banū al-Naḍīr and inform them that Allah the Almighty has informed Me of the treachery (الغدر) you intended. Either you leave our land, or you declare war." They said: "We will leave your land." But 'Abd Allāh ibn Ubayy (عبد الله ابن أبيّ) sent word to them: "Do not leave, but stay and declare war against Muḥammad, for I and my people and my allies will support you. If you leave, I will leave with you, and if you fight, I will fight with you." So they stayed, repaired their fortresses (حصُونهم), and prepared for battle. They sent word to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "We will not leave, so do what you intend to do." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stood up, and his companions magnified [Allah] (كبّر). He said to the Commander of the Faithful عليه السّلام: "Advance toward Banū al-Naḍīr." The Commander of the Faithful عليه السّلام took the banner and advanced, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم came and surrounded their fortress. 'Abd Allāh ibn Ubayy betrayed them (غدر بهم). When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم appeared at the front of their houses, they fortified what was near them and destroyed what was adjacent to it. And the man among them who had a beautiful house (بيت حسن) was grieved by this. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had commanded the cutting down of their palm trees. They were distressed by this and said: "O Muḥammad, does Allah command you to commit corruption (الفساد)? If this [land] belongs to you, then take it, but if it belongs to us, do not cut it down." When some time passed after that, they said: "O Muḥammad, we will leave your land, so give us what is ours." He said: "No, but you will leave, and you will have what the camels can carry." They did not accept that, so they remained for some days. Then they said: "We will leave, and we will have what the camels can carry." He said: "No one among you shall carry anything. Whoever we find with anything of that, we will kill him." So they departed upon that condition. A group of them settled in Fadak (فدك) and Wādī al-Qurā (وادي القرى), and a group of them departed to Syria (الشام). So Allah revealed concerning this: هُوَ الَّذِي أَخْرَجَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** [and the rest of] the verses. ** ما ظَنَنْتُمْ أَنْ يَخْرُجُوا ** (3) You did not think that they would leave, due to the severity of their might (بأسهم) and their strength (منعتهم). وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ مانِعَتُهُمْ حُصُونُهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ and they thought that their fortresses would protect them from Allah. Meaning: that their fortresses would protect them from the might (بأس) of Allah. فَأَتاهُمُ اللهُ but Allah came upon them Meaning: His punishment (عذابه), which was the terror (الرّعب) and the compulsion (الاضطرار) to exile (الجلاء).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السّلام: Meaning, He sent a punishment upon them. ** مِنْ حَيْثُ لَمْ يَحْتَسِبُوا ** from where they did not reckon, due to the strength of their confidence (وثوقهم). وَقَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الرُّعْبَ ** and He cast terror into their hearts. And He established within them the fear (الخوف) which terrifies them (يرعبها), meaning, fills them [with dread]. يُخْرِبُونَ بُيُوتَهُمْ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ ** They ruined their houses with their own hands, out of stinginess (ضنّا) regarding them for the Muslims, and to remove the furnishings (آلاتها) they deemed valuable. وَأَيْدِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** and the hands of the faithful. And the faithful also ruined their exteriors (ظواهرها) as an act of vengeance (نكاية) and to widen the field of battle (مجال القتال). And [the phrase] is connected to "their hands" (أَيْدِيهِمْ) because the destruction by the faithful was caused by their hatred [of the Jews], so it is as if [the Jews] employed them in it. And [the word] يُخَرِّبُونَ (يخرّبون) is read with tashdīd (التّشديد), which is more emphatic. فَاعْتَبِرُوا يا أُولِي الْأَبْصارِ So take a lesson, O you who have insight! So be admonished by their condition, and do not commit treachery (تغدروا), and do not rely upon other than Allah.

(٣) وَلَوْ لا أَنْ كَتَبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْجَلاءَ ** (3) And had not Allah ordained exile for them The departure (الخروج) from their homelands, He would have punished them in this world with killing and captivity (السبّى), just as He did with Banu Qurayzah. وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابُ النَّارِ ** and there is the punishment of the Fire for them in the Hereafter. Meaning, that if they escaped the punishment of this world, they would not escape the punishment of the Hereafter.

(٤) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَاقُّوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَمَنْ يُشَاقِّ اللهَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ. (4) That is because they defied Allah and His Apostle, and whoever defies Allah—Allah is indeed severe in retribution. (٥) ما قَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ لِينَةٍ ** (5) Whatever palm tree you cut down A noble palm tree (نخلة كريمة). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means the al-Ajwah (العجوّة), which is the mother of dates (أمّ التمر), and it is the one which Allah sent down from Paradise for Adam. ** أَوْ تَرَكْتُمُوها قائِمَةً عَلى أُصُولِها فَبِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** or left standing on its roots, was by Allah’s leave, So, by His command (فبامره). Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning what they reproached him (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) for regarding the cutting of the palm trees. ** وَلِيُخْزِيَ الْفاسِقِينَ ** and that He might disgrace the transgressors. And He permitted you to cut (them) down so that He might recompense them for their transgression (فسقهم) with that which enraged them concerning it.

(٦) وَما أَفاءَ اللهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ ** (6) And what Allah restored to His Apostle meaning, He returned it to him. For indeed, all that is between the heaven and the earth belongs to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and to His Apostle, and to their followers among the believers who are characterized by what Allah described them with in His saying: التَّائِبُونَ الْعابِدُونَ (The penitent, the worshippers...) [up to] the end of the Ayah. Thus, whatever of it is in the hands of the polytheists (المشركين), the disbelievers (الكفّار), the oppressors (الظّلمة), and the wicked (الفجّار), that is their right, which Allah restored (أفاء) to them and returned it to them. This is according to what is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith recorded in al-Kafi. ** مِنْهُمْ ** from them meaning, from Banu al-Nadir. فَما أَوْجَفْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** for which you did not spur meaning, you did not make run quickly for its acquisition, derived from al-wajif (الوَجِيف) which is the speed of travel. مِنْ خَيْلٍ وَلا رِكابٍ ** any horse or riding camel meaning, what is ridden among the camels (الإبل), where the term (فِيل) became dominant [for the spoils]. And that is because their villages were two miles from Medina, so they walked to them on foot, except for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for he rode a camel or a donkey, and no further fighting took place. For this reason, the Ansar were not given anything from it, except for two or three men who were in need. وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يُسَلِّطُ رُسُلَهُ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ but Allah gives His apostles power over whomever He wishes. He casts terror into their hearts. وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ and Allah is able to do all things, so He does what He wills, sometimes through apparent means (الوسائط الظاهرة) and sometimes without them.

(٧) ما أَفاءَ اللهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرى ** (7) What Allah has restored to His Apostle from the people of the townships This is an explanation (بيان) for the first [clause], and that is why it was not conjoined [with the following clause]: فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ. (it belongs to Allah and the Apostle, and the near relatives, the orphans, the needy and the traveller.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "We, by Allah, are those whom Allah meant by 'the near relatives' (ذي القربى), whom Allah joined with Himself and His Prophet, for He said: ما أَفاءَ اللهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرى فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ ** (What Allah has restored to His Apostle from the people of the townships, it belongs to Allah and the Apostle, and the near relatives, the orphans, the needy and the traveller.)—specifically from among us. And He did not assign a share for us in charity (الصّدقة). Allah honored His Prophet and honored us by not feeding us the filth (أوساخ) of what is in the hands of the people."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "[The verse refers to] our near relatives (قرباؤنا), our orphans (أيتامنا), our needy (مساكيننا), and our travellers (أبناء سبيلنا)." He said: "And all the jurists (الفقهاء) said that they are the orphans of the people generally (عامّة), and likewise the needy (المساكين) and the travellers (أبناء السّبيل)." He said: "And that has also been narrated from them (peace be upon them). The full discussion on this has already preceded in Surat al-Anfal."

كَيْ لا يَكُونَ دُولَةً بَيْنَ الْأَغْنِياءِ مِنْكُمْ lest it should circulate among the rich among you. Lest the fay' (الفيء) should be something that the rich circulate (يتداوله) and pass around among themselves, as was the case in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية).

And it was read (وقرء) as takūnu (تكون) with the tā' (التّاء) and dawlatun (دولة) with the raf' (الرّفع) [nominative case].

وَما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ ** (Take whatever the Apostle gives you, and refrain from whatever he forbids you, and be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is severe in retribution.) وَما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ ** (And whatever the Apostle gives you) of command (الامر) فَخُذُوهُ ** (take it) so adhere to it (فتمسّكوا به). وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ ** (And whatever he forbids you from) from performing it (عن إتيانه) فَانْتَهُوا ** (refrain) from it. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be wary of Allah) regarding opposing the Apostle of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. إِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ ** (Indeed Allah is severe in retribution) for whoever opposes [Him].

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): اتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (Be wary of Allah) regarding the oppression (ظلم) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). إِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ ** (Indeed Allah is severe in retribution) for whoever oppresses them.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, disciplined His Apostle until He established him upon what He willed, then He delegated authority (فوّض) to him. Then He, Mighty is His mention, said: وَما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا ** (Take whatever the Apostle gives you, and refrain from whatever he forbids you). Thus, whatever Allah delegated to His Apostle, He has delegated to us." And in a narration: "He delegated the affair of His creation to His Prophet so that He might observe how their obedience is." Then he recited this verse. The narrations (الأخبار) regarding this meaning are numerous. And in some of them, it is added: "So Allah forbade wine (الخمر) specifically, and the Apostle of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade every intoxicant (كلّ مسكر). So Allah permitted that for him, and He did not delegate authority (يفوّض) to any of the Prophets other than him." And in some of them, other things that Allah permitted are enumerated.

(٨) لِلْفُقَراءِ الْمُهاجِرِينَ ** (8) for the poor emigrants those who emigrated from Mecca (مكّة) to Medina (المدينة) and from the abode of war (دار الحرب) to the abode of Islam (دار الإسلام). It is said that it is a substitute (بدل) for لِذِي الْقُرْبى ** (for the relatives) and what is conjoined to it. And whoever gave to the wealthy among the relatives (ذوى القربى), he specified the substitution (الابدال) with what follows it. And the fay' (الفيء) refers to the fay' (فيء) of Banu al-Nadir (بنى النّضير) الَّذِينَ أُخْرِجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ وَأَمْوالِهِمْ who were expelled from their homes and their possessions, The disbelievers (كفّار) of Mecca (مكّة) expelled them and took their possessions. يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلاً مِنَ اللهِ وَرِضْواناً وَيَنْصُرُونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** seeking grace from Allah and [His] pleasure, and helping Allah and His Apostle. with their souls and their possessions. أُولئِكَ هُمُ الصَّادِقُونَ ** It is they who are the truthful. in their faith (ايمانهم).

(٩) وَالَّذِينَ تَبَوَّؤُا الدَّارَ وَالْإِيمانَ ** (And those who made their home in the City and in faith) This phrase is an 'atf (conjunction/coordination) upon al-Muhajirun (the Emigrants), or it is a new sentence (استيناف) whose predicate (خبره) is يُحِبُّونَ (they love), since nothing from the spoils (الفيء) was apportioned to them. Those intended by this are al-Ansar (the Helpers), for they adhered to Medina and faith (الْإِيمانَ) and established themselves firmly in both. It is also said that they made their home in the Abode of Emigration (دار الهجرة) and the Abode of Faith (دار الايمان). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْإِيمانَ ** (the faith)—some of it is from some, and it is an abode (دار). Likewise, Islam is an abode (دار), and disbelief (الكفر) is an abode (دار). ** مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (before them) meaning, before the emigration of al-Muhajirun (the Emigrants). يُحِبُّونَ مَنْ هاجَرَ إِلَيْهِمْ ** (love those who migrate to them) and it does not weigh heavily upon them. وَلا يَجِدُونَ فِي صُدُورِهِمْ حاجَةً مِمَّا أُوتُوا ** (and do not find in their hearts any need for what they have been given) meaning, from what al-Muhajirun were given of the spoils (الفيء) and other things. وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (and prefer [them] over themselves) meaning, they give precedence over themselves. وَلَوْ كانَ بِهِمْ خَصاصَةٌ ** (even if they should be in need) meaning, poverty and necessity. وَمَنْ يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِ ** (And those who are saved from the avarice of their own souls) even to the extent that he opposes it in what dominates it, such as the love of wealth and the hatred of spending. فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** (it is they who are the felicitous) meaning, those who attain success (الفائزون) through immediate praise (الثناء العاجل) and deferred reward (الثواب الأجل). In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): al-Shuhh (avarice) is more severe than al-Bukhl (stinginess). Indeed, the stingy person (البخيل) is stingy with what is in his hand, while the avaricious person (الشحّيح) is avaricious regarding what is in the hands of people and what is in his own hands, to the extent that he does not see anything in the hands of people except that he wishes it were his, whether through lawful (الحلّ) or unlawful (الحرام) means, and he is not content with what Allah has provided Him. In al-Amali, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: A man came to him and complained to him of hunger. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله sent word to the houses of his wives, and they said: "We have nothing but water." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله then said: "Who will take care of this man tonight?" Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) said: "I will take care of him, O Messenger of Allah." He went to Fatimah (peace be upon her) and said to her: "What do you have, O daughter of the Messenger of Allah?" She replied: "We have nothing but the evening meal (قوت العشّية), but we will prefer our guest." He said: "O daughter of Muhammad, put the children to sleep (نوّمى الصبيّة) and extinguish the lamp (أطفئ المصباح)." When Ali (peace be upon him) awoke in the morning, he went to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and informed him of the matter. He had not yet departed when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (and prefer [them] over themselves) [the rest of] the Ayah. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He said to the people after the death of Umar ibn al-Khattab, in a narration enumerating virtues (المناقب): "I adjure you by Allah, is there anyone among you concerning whom this Ayah was revealed— وَيُؤْثِرُونَ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ (and prefer [them] over themselves) [the rest of] the Ayah—other than me?" They said: "No."

(١٠) وَالَّذِينَ جاؤُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ** (10) And those who came after them (who came) after the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار), encompassing all other believers. يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنا وَلِإِخْوانِنَا الَّذِينَ سَبَقُونا بِالْإِيمانِ ** say, 'Our Lord! Forgive us and our brethren who preceded us in faith, meaning: our brethren in religion (الذّين). وَلا تَجْعَلْ فِي قُلُوبِنا غِلًّا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** and do not put any rancour in our hearts toward those who have faith. meaning: hatred (حقدا) for them. رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ رَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ ** Our Lord! You are indeed most kind and merciful.' Thus, it is fitting that You answer our supplication.

(١١) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ نافَقُوا ** (Have you not regarded those who are hypocrites) Al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning Ibn Ubayy and his companions. يَقُولُونَ لِإِخْوانِهِمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ ** meaning Banu al-Nadir (بنى النّضير). لَئِنْ أُخْرِجْتُمْ ** from your homes, we shall surely leave with you, and we shall never obey anyone concerning you—in fighting you or abandoning you—ever. meaning, from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and the Muslims. وَإِنْ قُوتِلْتُمْ لَنَنْصُرَنَّكُمْ وَاللهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ** due to His knowledge that they will not do that.

(١٢) لَئِنْ أُخْرِجُوا لا يَخْرُجُونَ مَعَهُمْ وَلَئِنْ قُوتِلُوا لا يَنْصُرُونَهُمْ ** (12) If they are expelled, they will not go out with them, and if they are fought, they will not help them. And that was the case, for Ibn Ubayy and his companions sent word to Banu al-Nadir with that (promise), then they failed them, as was mentioned at the beginning of the Surah. وَلَئِنْ نَصَرُوهُمْ ** (And if they help them) on the assumption and estimation (i.e., hypothetically), لَيُوَلُّنَّ الْأَدْبارَ ** (they will surely turn their backs) fleeing in defeat (انهزاما). ثُمَّ لا يُنْصَرُونَ ** (then they will not be helped) afterwards.

(13) ** لَأَنْتُمْ أَشَدُّ رَهْبَةً ** (You are) a source of awe (مرهوبين) in their hearts (صُدُورِهِمْ), for they used to conceal their fear of the believers, مِنَ اللهِ ** over what they manifest as hypocrisy. ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** (You are a greater source of awe in their hearts than Allah. That is because they are a people who do not understand.) They do not know the greatness of Allah so that they might fear Him with the fear that is His due, and know that He is the One Who deserves to be feared.

(14) ** لَا يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ ** the Jews and the hypocrites, all of them, gathered together ** إِلَّا فِي قُرًى مُحَصَّنَةٍ ** with pathways (الدروب) and trenches (الخنادق) ** أَوْ مِنْ وَرَاءِ جُدُرٍ ** due to the excessiveness of their dread. And it was read (جدار) (singular wall). لَا يُقَاتِلُونَكُمْ جَمِيعًا إِلَّا فِي قُرًى مُحَصَّنَةٍ أَوْ مِنْ وَرَاءِ جُدُرٍ (They will not fight you collectively except in fortified towns or from behind walls.)

** بَأْسُهُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ شَدِيدٌ ** meaning, that is not due to their weakness or cowardice, for their might becomes intense when some of them fight others. Rather, it is because Allah cast dread (الرغب) into their hearts, and because the brave man becomes a coward and the mighty man is humiliated when he fights Allah and His Messenger. بَأْسُهُمْ بَيْنَهُمْ شَدِيدٌ (Their might is intense among themselves.)

** تَحْسَبُهُمْ جَمِيعاً ** gathered together, in agreement ** وَقُلُوبُهُمْ شَتَّى ** scattered, due to the divergence of their beliefs and the difference in their aims. تَحْسَبُهُمْ جَمِيعًا وَقُلُوبُهُمْ شَتَّى (You suppose them to be united, while their hearts are diverse.)

** ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ ** to what is beneficial for them, and that the scattering of hearts weakens their strength. ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (That is because they are a people who do not apply reason.)

(١٥) كَمَثَلِ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (15) (Like the case of those who were before them) al-Qummi said: Meaning Banu Qaynuqa' (بني قينقاع). قَرِيباً ** (recently) in a near time, they tasted the evil consequence (وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ), which is the bad outcome of their disbelief (كُفْرِهِمْ) in this world (الدنيا). وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** (and there is a painful punishment for them) in the Hereafter (الآخرة).

(١٦) كَمَثَلِ الشَّيْطانِ ** (16) (Like the case of Satan) Meaning: the likeness of the hypocrites (المنافقين) in inciting the Jews (اليهود) to fight, and then their withdrawal (نُكُوصِهِمْ), is like the case of Satan. al-Qummi said: Allah set forth a parable concerning Ibn Ubayy (ابن أبي) and Banu al-Nadir (بني النضير), and He said: كَمَثَلِ الشَّيْطانِ (Like the case of Satan). إِذْ قالَ لِلْإِنْسانِ اكْفُرْ ** (when he said to man, ‘Disbelieve!’) He incited him to disbelief (الكُفْر) with the incitement of one who commands the commanded. فَلَمَّا كَفَرَ قالَ إِنِّي (But when he disbelieved, he said, ‘I am) بَرِيءٌ مِنْكَ ** (quit of you’) He disavowed him for fear that he might share in the punishment (العَذَاب) with him, and that did not benefit him. And He said: إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ رَبَّ الْعالَمِينَ.** (‘Indeed, I fear Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’)

(١٧) فَكانَ عاقِبَتَهُما أَنَّهُما فِي النَّارِ خالِدَيْنِ فِيها وَذلِكَ جَزاءُ الظَّالِمِينَ. (17) Thus the outcome for both of them was that they will be in the Fire, abiding in it, and that is the requital of the wrongdoers. (١٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَلْتَنْظُرْ نَفْسٌ ما قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ ** (18) O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and let every soul observe what it has sent ahead for tomorrow. [Meaning] the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). He named it thus due to its proximity, or because the world is like a day and the Hereafter is its tomorrow. And its indefiniteness (تَنكِير) is for magnification (تَعظِيم). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (And be wary of Allah) is a repetition (تَكرِير) for emphasis (تَأكِيد). إِنَّ اللهَ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** (Indeed, Allah is well aware of what you do), and this is like a threat (وَعِيد) regarding acts of disobedience (المَعَاصِي).

** وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ نَسُوا اللهَ ** (19) And do not be like those who forgot Allah They forgot His right (حقّه). فَأَنْساهُمْ أَنْفُسَهُمْ ** so He made them forget their own souls. So He made them forgetful of them [their souls], until they did not hear what benefits them and did not do what saves them. أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ ** It is they who are the transgressors. The complete ones in transgression (الفسوق).

** لا يَسْتَوِي أَصْحابُ النَّارِ وَأَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ ** (20) The inmates of the Fire and the inhabitants of paradise are not equal. Those who debased (استمهنوا) their souls, thus deserving the Fire, and those who perfected them, thus becoming worthy (استأهلوا) of Paradise. **أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمُ الْفائِزُونَ ** The inhabitants of paradise are the triumphant ones. by means of the everlasting bliss.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): That the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited this verse and said: أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ ** The inhabitants of paradise are those who obeyed me and submitted (وسلّم) to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) after me and affirmed (وأقرّ) his wilayah (بولايته). And أَصْحابُ النَّارِ ** the inmates of the Fire are those who were displeased with (سخط) the wilayah (الولاية), broke the covenant (ونقض العهد), and fought him after me.

** لَوْ أَنْزَلْنا هذَا الْقُرْآنَ عَلى جَبَلٍ لَرَأَيْتَهُ خاشِعاً مُتَصَدِّعاً مِنْ خَشْيَةِ اللهِ ** (21) Had We sent down this Quran upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled, split open for the fear of Allah.

split open because of it (the fear). It was said: [It is] a metaphor (تمثيل) and a hypothetical scenario (تخيّل), just as occurred concerning His saying: إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمانَةَ . And the intended meaning is the reprimand of man for his lack of awe (تخشّع) upon the recitation of the Quran, due to the hardness of his heart and his lack of contemplation (تدبّر). وَتِلْكَ الْأَمْثالُ نَضْرِبُها لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ. (22) These parables We strike for mankind, so that they may reflect.

(٢٢) هُوَ اللهُ الَّذِي لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ ** (22) He is Allah, there is no god except Him, Knower of the sensible and the insensible. It was said meaning that which is absent from the sense and that which is present to it, or the non-existent (المعدوم) and the existent (الموجود), or the secret (السرّ) and the manifest (العلانيّة). And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الْغَيْبِ ** (the Unseen) is that which has not been, and وَالشَّهادَةِ ** (the Seen) is that which has been. هُوَ الرَّحْمنُ الرَّحِيمُ. He is the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.**

** هُوَ اللهُ الَّذِي لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْمَلِكُ الْقُدُّوسُ ** (23) He is Allah, there is no god except Him, the Sovereign, the All-holy, (The meaning of الْقُدُّوسُ is) the One Who is perfect in purity from that which necessitates deficiency. Al-Qummi said: He is the One Who is free from the impurities of afflictions (الآفات) that necessitate ignorance. السَّلامُ ** the Peace, the Possessor of safety (السلامة) from every deficiency and affliction (آفة). الْمُؤْمِنُ ** the Maintainer of security, the Giver of security (الأمن). Al-Qummi said: He grants security (يؤمن) to His friends (أوليائه) from the punishment. الْمُهَيْمِنُ ** the Watcher, the Observer (الرقيب), the Guardian (الحافظ) of everything. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, the Witness (الشاهد). الْعَزِيزُ الْجَبَّارُ ** the All-mighty, the All-compeller, the One Whose will (مشيّته) is executed in everyone, and in Whom the will of no one is executed, and the One Who rectifies the conditions of His creation. (الْمُتَكَبِّرُ) the All-magnificent, the One Who is too great (تكبّر) for everything that necessitates need and deficiency. سُبْحانَ اللهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ. All-glorious is Allah above what they associate [with Him]!

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked what the interpretation (تفسير) of سُبْحانَ اللهِ ** (All-glorious is Allah) was. He replied: "It is the magnification of the Majesty (جلال) of Allah and His purification (تنزيهه) from what every polytheist (مشرك) has said concerning Him. When the servant says it, every angel invokes blessings upon him."

** هُوَ اللهُ الْخالِقُ الْبارِئُ الْمُصَوِّرُ ** (24) He is Allah, the Creator, the Maker, the Former. Everything that emerges from non-existence (العدم) into existence (الوجود) requires, firstly, determination (تقدير); secondly, bringing into existence (الإيجاد) in accordance with that determination; and thirdly, forming (التصوير) after bringing into existence. Thus, Allah, glory be to Him, is الخالق (al-Khāliq), البارئ (al-Bāriʾ), and المصوّر (al-Muṣawwir) according to these three considerations (الاعتبارات). لَهُ الْأَسْماءُ الْحُسْنى ** His belong the Best Names. which indicate the beauties of meanings (المعاني).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, has ninety-nine Names—that is, one hundred minus one—whoever enumerates them (أحصاها) shall enter Paradise." Then He mentioned those Names.

Our Shaykh al-Saduq said: Enumerating them (إحصاؤها) means encompassing them (الإحاطة بها) and understanding their meanings (معانيها), and the meaning of enumeration (الإحصاء) is not merely counting them.

I say: We have mentioned other meanings for this hadith and have interpreted every single Name in our book called ‘Ilm al-Yaqīn (علم اليقين). Whoever desires them must refer to it. يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** All that is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him, due to His transcendence (تنزّهه) above all deficiencies (النقايص). وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ (and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.) the One Who encompasses every perfection, since all things are subsumed under His Power (القدرة) and Knowledge (العلم).

In Thawāb al-A‘māl and al-Majma‘, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "Whoever recites Sūrat al-Ḥashr, there will remain neither Paradise, nor Hellfire, nor the Throne (العرش), nor the Footstool (الكرسيّ), nor the Veil (الحجاب), nor the seven heavens, nor the seven earths, nor the atmosphere (الهواء), nor the wind (الريح), nor the birds (الطير), nor the trees (الشجر), nor the mountains (الجبال), nor the beasts (الدوابّ), nor the sun (الشمس), nor the moon (القمر), nor the angels (الملائكة), except that they will pray for him (صلّوا عليه) and seek forgiveness for him (استغفروا له). And if he dies on that day or night, he will die as a martyr (شهيداً), if Allah wills."
